claims.text: Alex Hormozi advises that for a local pool construction business using paid search, calling inbound leads within 60 seconds of submission is the critical response-time target; he positions this as the core operational lever alongside ad spend.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommends running $5,000 per month in PPC as the paid acquisition baseline for a new local pool construction business in a market like Florida, treating paid search as the primary demand-generation channel rather than organic or referral.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: founder has industry license or access

numbers.axis: monthly PPC spend for new local home-services business

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommends structuring the initial sale for a local pool service business as a $1,500 upfront package, then immediately rolling the customer into a recurring continuity offering after that first transaction, rather than treating the initial sale as a one-time project.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: local pool construction or maintenance business
claims.mechanism: The upfront payment establishes a customer relationship and commitment; continuity is added immediately while the customer is in buying mode, avoiding a re-sell from scratch later.

numbers.axis: initial offer price for local pool service entry package

anchors.region: recommended upfront package (pool construction, new customer)

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommends immediately following the initial upfront sale with a continuity (recurring) offer rather than waiting for a second touchpoint, treating the post-purchase moment as the optimal upsell window.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: local home-services business (pool construction or maintenance)
customer has just completed initial purchase
claims.mechanism: Customer is already in a commitment state post-purchase; introducing continuity immediately captures momentum before the buyer exits the decision frame.

examples.text: A 17-year-old asked for advice on starting a pool construction business, noting his father holds a construction license in Florida and that he had done digital marketing for a pool company for a year. Alex Hormozi responded with a complete four-step playbook: run $5,000/month in PPC, call every lead within 60 seconds, sell a $1,500 upfront package, then roll the customer immediately into a continuity offering. The advice treats PPC + fast response + upfront capture + continuity sequencing as the minimum viable go-to-market for a new local service business.

practices.text: For a new local pool construction business, lead with PPC as the primary acquisition channel rather than relying on organic search, referrals, or social media.
Structure the first customer transaction as an upfront cash payment before work begins, not as a post-delivery invoice.
